req.title: Approve leave request
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) the happy-path where the correct manager approves an open request — covered by EP on valid inputs; (2) a 403 error when a manager who is NOT the assignee tries to approve — covered by NEG/DT; (3) a 409 conflict when the request is already actioned — covered by STC (invalid state transition); (4) a boundary case where approval happens on exactly day 5 (the last permissible business day before auto-escalation) — covered by BVA. Together these achieve positive, negative, and boundary coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_pending_request
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending and is assigned to this manager
Employee account exists and can receive notifications
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the request id
Query notification service for the employee
expected: Audit log contains one entry for this approval action
trace: R-WEB-062: manager clicks approve -> request state=approved
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_pending_request(
    manager_api_client,        # <fixture> authenticated as assignee manager
    pending_leave_request,     # <fixture> returns {id, employee_id, state='pending'}
    audit_log_query,           # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> list[dict]
    notification_query,        # <helper_fn>(employee_id) -> list[dict]
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    emp_id = pending_leave_request["employee_id"]

    resp = manager_api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "approved"

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(req_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approved" for e in audit_entries)

    notifications = notification_query(emp_id)
    assert any(n["event"] == "leave_approved" for n in notifications)

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager but is NOT the assignee of the leave request
Leave request exists with state=pending assigned to a different manager
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with credentials of a manager who is NOT the assignee
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating insufficient permission or wrong assignee
Leave request state remains pending (no state change)
trace: R-WEB-062: error pathway - 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    other_manager_api_client,  # <fixture> manager authenticated but NOT the assignee
    pending_leave_request,     # <fixture> assigned to a different manager
    get_leave_request,         # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> dict
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]

    resp = other_manager_api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

    current_state = get_leave_request(req_id)["state"]
    assert current_state == "pending"

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and IS the assignee
Leave request exists with state=approved (already actioned)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve a second time with the same manager credentials
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating request already actioned
Leave request state remains approved (idempotent guard, no corruption)
trace: R-WEB-062: error pathway - 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-062: invalid state transition approved->approved must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    manager_api_client,        # <fixture> authenticated as assignee manager
    approved_leave_request,    # <fixture> returns request already in state=approved
    get_leave_request,         # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> dict
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request["id"]

    resp = manager_api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

    final_state = get_leave_request(req_id)["state"]
    assert final_state == "approved"

tests.name: test_approve_on_day_5_boundary_is_accepted_and_not_escalated
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and IS the assignee
Leave request created exactly 5 business days ago (boundary: last valid day before auto-escalation)
Request state=pending; auto-escalation job has NOT yet run for this request
actions: Freeze/mock system time so that today == request_created_date + 5 business days
Capture HTTP response status, body, and escalation flag
expected: Escalation flag on the request remains false / no escalation record created
Audit log contains approval entry with timestamp within day 5
trace: R-WEB-062: unactioned requests auto-escalate after 5 business days; approval on day 5 must succeed
R-WEB-062: BVA upper boundary of the 5-business-day window
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_on_day_5_boundary_is_accepted_and_not_escalated(
    manager_api_client,          # <fixture> authenticated as assignee manager
    pending_leave_request_day5,  # <fixture> request created exactly 5 biz days ago
    frozen_clock,                # <fixture> freezes time at day-5 boundary
    get_leave_request,           # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> dict
    escalation_query,            # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> list[dict]
    audit_log_query,             # <helper_fn>(request_id) -> list[dict]
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request_day5["id"]

    with frozen_clock(days_offset=5):
        resp = manager_api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    escalations = escalation_query(req_id)
    assert len(escalations) == 0

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(req_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approved" for e in audit_entries)
